title: Mission Report
description: The structured return one parallel agent (sortie) produces for its seam. Used directly as the Claude Code Workflow agent(prompt, {schema}) StructuredOutput, so the agent is FORCED to self-report against its Mission Brief. The coherence-gate Stage A diffs this report against the brief (invariants, non_goals, reference_frame, contract). Source of truth for the MissionReport dataclass (formation/formation.py).

seam_id.description: Echoes the brief's seam_id. The gate keys reports to briefs by this.

summary.description: One paragraph: what this sortie produced.

touched_paths.description: EVERY path created or modified by this sortie. Stage A intersects touched_paths across sibling reports to detect mid-air collision (two agents that mutated the same file outside their seams).

verification.description: Result of running the brief's verification_rubric.verification_command. The agent MUST actually run it.

ran.description: False if the agent could not run it — itself a finding.

passed.description: True only if the command exited 0.

vocabulary_terms.description: The key terms the agent used for the brief's concepts. Stage A diffs these against reference_frame.canonical_terms / forbidden_synonyms to detect vocabulary drift.

invariants_selfcheck.description: One entry per brief invariant: did it hold? Self-report is a signal, not proof — the gate corroborates with verification + adversarial verify for high-lambda seams.

non_goals_selfcheck.description: One entry per brief non_goal: was it respected (untouched)?

contract_changed.description: True if the sortie altered its FROZEN interface_contract. Any true here is a contract breach the gate escalates — a seam cannot change its own boundary unilaterally.

notes.description: Anything the gate or a human should know — ambiguities hit, decisions deferred, disambiguation signals raised.
